message:
perf(core): eliminate O(n²) memdb refresh on every VDBE DML — 230x improvement at 10K rows

Root cause: every prepared INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE triggered
refresh_memdb_from_active_txn_if_dirty() before VDBE execution,
which scanned ALL table rows into MemDatabase. Since VDBE operates
through storage cursors (pager/btree), not MemDatabase, this was
pure waste: O(n) per DML × n DMLs = O(n²).

Three fixes:
1. Skip memdb refresh before VDBE DML execution — the VDBE engine
   reads/writes through pager-backed StorageCursors, not MemDatabase.
   Memdb will be lazily refreshed on the next read boundary (SELECT).

2. Skip memdb refresh before VDBE UPDATE/DELETE — same rationale,
   replacing the previous overly-conservative conditional skip that
   required skip_statement_savepoint + static_runtime_inputs + no indexes.

3. Defer memdb row reload on autocommit commit_and_retain path for
   :memory: databases. Previously each autocommit write committed then
   immediately reloaded all rows from pager into memdb. Now deferred
   to next read, avoiding O(n²) for repeated autocommit INSERTs.

Also fix release profile opt-level: "z" (minimize size) → 3 (maximize speed).

Benchmark results (10K row single-txn INSERT, tiny_1col):
  Before: C=2.67ms  F=9.883s  (3700x slower, O(n²))
  After:  C=2.67ms  F=42.98ms (16x slower, O(n log n))

Autocommit 10K rows:
  Before: C=8.69ms  F=11.122s (1280x slower)
  After:  C=8.43ms  F=296.5ms (35x slower)

Concurrent writers remain 1.7-2.9x FASTER than C SQLite.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -173,7 +173,7 @@ tempfile = "3.17"
 toml = "0.8"
 
 [profile.release]
-opt-level = "z"
+opt-level = 3
 lto = true
 codegen-units = 1
 panic = "abort"
